resources.string: Assalamualaikum, I\'m inviting you to download one of the best Islamic apps without any ads or interruptions: https://play.google.com/store/apps/details?id=com.hazrat.islam24
Determining the time of \'Asr - the method of the jurists
Thank you for your feedback. We appreciate your input.
Thank you for your positive feedback! We\'d love for you to rate us.
Nisab is a threshold, referring to the minimum amount of wealth
The nisab threshold for silver is %1$sgm (52.50 Tola/Vori) or the cash equivalent
To calculate nisab, enter the current value of silver/gm:

Zakat on Gold should be calculated at 2.5% of the current market value of the gold you own as on the date of valuation. This applies to gold in all forms, whether it\'s jewelry, coins, or bars, provided it reaches or exceeds the Nisab threshold.
Zakat on money should be calculated at 2.5% of your total monetary assets as on the date of valuation. This includes various forms of wealth that are liquid or easily accessible, such as:
• International Currency (Foreign Money in Your Possession)
• Loans Given (Money owed to you by others)
Ensure you include all liquid assets when calculating Zakat on your money. The total amount should be valued at 2.5%.

Monthly Living Cost refers to the total expenses you need to cover essential needs and live comfortably within a month. These are the basic expenses you should consider when calculating Zakat. Below are some key points to help you understand what to include:
Housing Expenses: Rent/mortgage, utilities, home maintenance.
Food & Groceries: Monthly food and household supplies.
Transportation: Costs like fuel, car payments, or public transport.
Healthcare: Health insurance, medications, and medical visits.
Education: Tuition fees, school supplies for children or yourself.
Communication & Internet: Costs for phone, internet, and communication services.
Miscellaneous: Essential recurring costs like child or elderly care.
Note: Exclude luxury expenses such as vacations, luxury goods, or excessive entertainment.

Zakat on Silver should be calculated at 2.5% of the current market value of the silver you own as on the date of valuation. This applies to silver in any form, including jewelry, coins, or bars, provided it reaches or exceeds the Nisab threshold.

Total Debt on You refers to the total amount you owe to others, including personal loans, credit card debt, mortgages, or any other financial obligations. When calculating Zakat, it is essential to consider this amount, as it can be deducted from your total assets. This ensures that Zakat is only paid on the net wealth that you truly own after accounting for your debts.

If you have any outstanding loans or debts, reduce the total amount of debt from your assets before calculating Zakat. This provides an accurate picture of your net wealth, ensuring that Zakat is only paid on the wealth free from obligations.
Otp send successfully check your email.

The Quran contains information about scientific concepts that were not known at the time of its revelation, such as the Theory of Relativity, the Big Bang Theory, genetics, black holes, and more. This demonstrates the compatibility between the Quran and science.
The Quran’s verses often encourage contemplation and exploration of the natural world, inspiring Muslims to pursue scientific knowledge and contribute to advancements in various fields.
Reading the Quran can help individuals find harmony between their faith and scientific understanding, fostering a holistic worldview that embraces both religious beliefs and scientific principles.

Research suggests that reciting and contemplating upon the verses of the Quran can help reduce stress levels. The rhythmic tone and soothing melodies in its recitation have a calming effect on both mind and body, promoting relaxation and reducing anxiety.

Studies show that regular Quranic reading improves cognitive function, including attention span, memory retention, and problem-solving skills. The complex linguistic structure of Arabic used in the Quran enhances brain activity, stimulating intellectual growth.

Engaging with the words of Allah through Quranic reading stimulates positive emotions such as happiness, gratitude, and contentment. This emotional well-being is linked to improved mental health outcomes like reduced symptoms of depression and enhanced overall life satisfaction.

When reading the Quran with Tajweed, individuals learn to pronounce each letter accurately and apply correct intonation. This leads to improved recitation skills and a more melodic rendition of the verses.
Tajweed helps learners grasp the meaning behind each word in the Quran. By following proper pronunciation guidelines, readers can fully comprehend the message conveyed by Allah (SWT) in His book.
This service allows the app to monitor volume key presses.
